about.ABOUT_CHARITY.paragraphs.0: We are a full service travel agency that provides customized excellence for the tourism needs of business and leisure clients.
about.ABOUT_CHARITY.paragraphs.1: Zakher Travel officially started its activity in 2016 and soon managed to make an outstanding achievement. We received thousands of tourists from the GCC region and other locations, helping to introduce them to the charming treasures of Azerbaijan, Türkiye, Georgia, Russia, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Uzbekistan, Poland, Albania, Hungary, Czechia, Slovakia, Germany, Ukraine, UAE.
about.ABOUT_CHARITY.paragraphs.2: As a tour operator, we established our cooperation with more than 800 travel companies through official contracts. Our professional and friendly staff of 100 people provide tailor-made holidays to our customers.
about.ABOUT_CHARITY.title: Charity
about.ABOUT_HERO.subtitle: Your online and offline supplier in the world since 2016 — professional travel agency in Azerbaijan.
about.ABOUT_HERO.title: Zakher Travel
about.ABOUT_INTRO_BODY.0: Zakher Travel Group of Companies was established in 2016 and soon managed to make an outstanding achievement in the travel industry. Over the years, we have welcomed thousands of visitors from the GCC region and beyond, crafting exceptional travel experiences across a diverse range of destinations — including Azerbaijan, Türkiye, Poland, Georgia, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Uzbekistan, Russia, the Czech Republic, Ukraine, and the UAE.
about.ABOUT_INTRO_BODY.1: Since our founding, we have expanded our presence by opening branches across several countries and building strong partnerships with more than 2,000 travel companies worldwide. Our team of over 50 experienced professionals specializes in designing tailor-made holidays and delivering high-quality travel services to meet the unique needs of every client.
about.ABOUT_INTRO_BODY.2: As a fully licensed tour operator, we take pride in bringing international travelers to our handpicked destinations. We place particular emphasis on promoting Azerbaijan — the Pearl of the Caucasus — where ancient history meets vibrant modern life. From breathtaking natural landscapes and impressive architectural landmarks to a rich and diverse cultural heritage, Azerbaijan offers an unforgettable travel experience.
about.ABOUT_INTRO_BODY.3: With four distinct seasons, each offering its own unique beauty and charm, our destinations can be enjoyed all year round. Travelers will always discover something new and inspiring, making the journey worth repeating.
about.ABOUT_MEMBERS.title: We Are Members Of
about.ABOUT_VALUES.subtitle: One management for 10 tourist destinations, a B2B online platform, online payments, and world-class service.
about.ABOUT_VALUES.title: WHY CHOOSE US?
about.DMC_PARTNER.paragraphs.0: With extensive local expertise and a trusted network of partners across Azerbaijan, Türkiye, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Uzbekistan, Georgia, Poland, Czech Republic, Russia, and the UAE, Zakher Travel delivers seamless destination management services tailored to the needs of international travel agencies, tour operators, corporate clients, and event organizers.
about.DMC_PARTNER.paragraphs.1: From MICE and incentive travel to luxury experiences, group tours, and corporate events, we provide end-to-end planning, reliable logistics, carefully selected suppliers, and on-the-ground operational support. Our regional presence enables us to create authentic, high-quality travel experiences while ensuring every journey is managed with professionalism, efficiency, and attention to detail.
about.DMC_PARTNER.title: Why Choose Zakher as Your DMC Partner?

about.WHO_WE_ARE_BODY.0: Zakher Travel Group of Companies officially started its activity in 2016 and soon managed to make an outstanding achievement. It received thousands of tourists from the GCC region and other countries and began to introduce them to the charming treasures not only of Azerbaijan, but also of countries such as Türkiye, Poland, Georgia, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Uzbekistan, Russia, the Czech Republic, Ukraine, and the UAE. Over the years of our existence, we have opened branches in these countries. We are a tour operator and we have contracts with more than 2,000 travel companies. We provide tailor-made holidays to our customers with our expert staff of 50 and more people and professional services.
about.WHO_WE_ARE_BODY.1: It is one of few companies that brings tourists from all over the world to visit our destinations. It does its utmost to introduce to tourists this historical and contemporary country, the Pearl of the Caucasus, characterized by charming nature, historical and architectural monuments, and rich cultural heritage.
about.WHO_WE_ARE_BODY.2: Tourists can visit our destinations any time of the year because of the distinctiveness of seasons and the fact that each season has its own climatic features. You can even visit more than once a year, and each time you'll find something new.

branches.details.0.description.0: Azerbaijan, often called the “Land of Fire,” is where East meets West and ancient civilizations have left their mark. From the bustling streets of Baku to historic towns and ancient monuments, the country offers a rich cultural experience.
branches.details.0.description.1: Its landscapes—from the Caspian Sea coastline to high plateaus, mountains, and lush valleys—create a harmony that enchants travelers with heritage, nature, and vibrant culture.
branches.details.0.title: Azerbaijan
branches.details.1.description.0: Often described as a bridge between East and West, Turkey is a land where civilizations have met for thousands of years. With a history stretching back over 10,000 years, it has been home to ancient empires whose traces are visible in its cities, monuments, and traditions.
branches.details.1.description.1: Turkey is rich in cultural heritage and historical depth. Its diverse landscapes range from Mediterranean and Aegean coastlines to high plateaus, mountains, and fertile valleys, creating a dynamic connection between nature, history, and modern life.
branches.details.1.title: Turkiye
branches.details.10.description.0: Ukraine, a land of fertile plains, majestic mountains, and vibrant rivers, reflects centuries of culture through its cities and countryside. Kyiv’s golden domes, Lviv’s charming streets, and the Carpathian Mountains highlight its natural beauty.
branches.details.10.description.1: The country is known for resilience, hospitality, and rich folk heritage.
branches.details.10.title: Ukraine
branches.details.11.description.0: Saudi Arabia, a land of deep heritage and rapid transformation, stands at the heart of the Arabian Peninsula. From Riyadh’s modern business districts to Jeddah’s Red Sea coastline and the Kingdom’s historic sites, the country offers a compelling blend of tradition, hospitality, and innovation.
branches.details.11.description.1: Through our Saudi Arabia branch, Zakher Travel supports international partners with destination management, MICE programs, group travel, and luxury itineraries across the Kingdom — backed by local expertise and reliable on-the-ground coordination.
branches.details.11.title: Saudi Arabia
branches.details.2.description.0: Kazakhstan, the heart of the Great Steppes, stretches across vast plains, deserts, serene lakes, and towering mountains. Here, nomadic heritage blends with modern urban life, creating a dynamic and multicultural society.
branches.details.2.description.1: Travelers are fascinated by its breathtaking nature, nomadic exoticism, and a striking modern contrast.
branches.details.2.title: Kazakhstan
branches.details.3.description.0: Often called the land of celestial mountains, Kyrgyzstan is an ancient homeland of nomadic culture with a history rooted in freedom and close connection to nature. The country is dominated by dramatic mountain ranges, alpine meadows, glaciers, and high-altitude lakes.
branches.details.3.description.1: These landscapes have shaped a culture based on mobility, endurance, and harmony with the environment. Kyrgyzstan preserves strong traditions of oral heritage, horsemanship, and communal values.
branches.details.3.title: Kyrgyzstan
branches.details.4.description.0: Uzbekistan, a jewel of the Great Silk Road, boasts cities like Samarkand, Bukhara, and Khiva, where stunning architecture and vibrant arts tell the story of a thriving past.
branches.details.4.description.1: Rich traditions and colorful history make every visit unforgettable. Travelers enjoy its history, art, and ancient culture.
branches.details.4.title: Uzbekistan
branches.details.5.contact.address: str Merab Kostava St, Tbilisi 0179, Georgia
branches.details.5.description.0: Georgia, with a history spanning over 8,000 years, offers landscapes ranging from the peaks of the Caucasus Mountains to fertile valleys and subtropical coasts. Ancient traditions live alongside modern life, creating a warm and resilient society.
branches.details.5.description.1: Georgia’s identity is deeply tied to its land, language, and historical continuity.
branches.details.5.title: Georgia
branches.details.6.description.0: Located in the heart of Europe, Poland has a long and complex history marked by resilience and cultural continuity. Its landscapes include vast plains, forests, lakes, and a Baltic Sea coastline, as well as mountain ranges in the south.
branches.details.6.description.1: Poland is rich in historical landmarks, intellectual traditions, and cultural heritage.
branches.details.6.title: Poland
branches.details.7.description.0: Known for its medieval cities and rich cultural legacy, the Czech Republic has been a center of art, philosophy, and craftsmanship for centuries. The country’s landscape is characterized by rolling hills, river valleys, forests, and historic towns integrated into the natural environment.
branches.details.7.description.1: This balance between nature and urban heritage contributes to its cultural refinement and architectural harmony.
branches.details.7.title: Czech Republic
branches.details.8.description.0: Spanning Eastern Europe and Northern Asia, Russia is one of the world’s most geographically vast and culturally complex countries. Its landscapes range from tundra and taiga forests to mountains, rivers, steppes, and Arctic coastlines.
branches.details.8.description.1: This immense natural diversity has shaped regional identities and historical development. Russia is known for its cultural depth, literary tradition and astonishing natural landscapes.
branches.details.8.title: Russia
branches.details.9.contact.address: Sharjah International Airport - منطقة حرة - Sharjah - UAE
branches.details.9.description.0: Known as a symbol of rapid development and innovation, the United Arab Emirates has transformed from a desert-based society into a global hub within a few decades. Its landscape is defined by vast deserts, coastal areas along the Arabian Gulf, and modern urban centers rising from arid land.
branches.details.9.description.1: Rooted in Bedouin traditions and Islamic values, the UAE reflects a balance between heritage, environment, and technological progress.
branches.details.9.title: United Arab Emirates

country.countries.0.description: Azerbaijan, often called the “Land of Fire,” is where East meets West and ancient civilizations have left their mark. From the bustling streets of Baku to historic towns and ancient monuments, the country offers a rich cultural experience. Its landscapes — from the Caspian Sea coastline to high plateaus, mountains, and lush valleys — create a harmony that enchants travelers with heritage, nature, and vibrant culture.
country.countries.0.faqs.0.answer:
Itinerary: Absheron – Ateshgah – Yanar Dag – Gala. Pick-up and drop-off at your hotel within Baku.
Highlights: Heydar Aliyev Center designed by Zaha Hadid, Ateshgah “Temple of Eternal Fire,” Yanardag — the eternally burning mountain, Gala Archaeological and Ethnographic Museum Complex, Bina Equestrian Center with the famous Karabakh horses, and Heydar Mosque — the biggest mosque in the Caucasus.
country.countries.0.faqs.0.question: Absheron Tour — 3 hours, private
country.countries.0.faqs.1.answer:
An independent night tour of Baku with a private driver and guide.
Highlights: Highland Park with panoramic views of Baku Bay, National Flag Square, Flame Towers, the Funicular, Carpet Museum, Baku Boulevard (Seaside Park), Little Venice, Government House, State Philharmonic Hall, and Fountain Square — the most famous street of Baku.
country.countries.0.faqs.1.question: Baku Night Tour — 2 hours, private
country.countries.0.faqs.10.answer:
Itinerary: Baku – Shemakha – Lahij – Baku (approx. 180 km one way, all season, 9–10 hours).
Visit Shemakha, the ancient capital of Shirvan, with Gulistan fortress, the Seven Domes mausoleum, and the 10th-century Juma Mosque. Then explore Lahij — a medieval high-altitude village founded in the 3rd–4th century BC, with cobbled streets, hereditary blacksmiths and coppersmiths, and a 1,500-year-old water system.
country.countries.0.faqs.10.question: Tour to the village of artisans — Lahij, 1 day
country.countries.0.faqs.11.answer: A full-day gastronomic journey: traditional breakfast in the Old City with tandir bread, honey, and cheese; the colorful eastern bazaar with spices, dried fruits, and Caspian black caviar; lunch with sturgeon or lamb kebab and shah-plov at an authentic restaurant; and a stroll through the Old Town with tea or wine tasting.
country.countries.0.faqs.11.question: Tastes and Flavors of Azerbaijani Cuisine — 5 hours
country.countries.0.faqs.12.answer: Contact us at incoming@zakher.travel or +994 12 310 09 32. Our team provides offline support by call, e-mail, and WhatsApp 24/7. Economy and VIP transport packages are available on request.

country.countries.0.faqs.2.answer:
A walking tour through medieval Icheri Sheher.
Highlights: Maiden Tower — the symbol of Baku, the 12th-century Juma Mosque, the Multani and Bukhara caravanserais, historic bathhouses and khanegahs, Shirvanshah’s Palace (12th–15th century), the Museum of Miniature Books, the Mugham Theatre, Fountain Square, the National History Museum, a sea trip along Baku Bay, and Baku’s shopping malls.
country.countries.0.faqs.2.question: Old City Excursion (Walking Tour) — 2.5 hours
country.countries.0.faqs.3.answer:
Itinerary: Baku – Gobustan – Mud Volcanoes – Baku (approx. 65 km one way, all season, Jeep 4×4).
Visit the Gobustan Museum of Petroglyphs — prehistoric rock drawings carved by primitive men in the Beyukdash, Kichikdash, Djingirdag and Shighgaya mountains — then trip to the mud volcanoes: about 350 of the world’s 800 mud volcanoes are found in Azerbaijan, most of them in Gobustan.
country.countries.0.faqs.3.question: Museum Gobustan and Mud Volcanoes Tour — 5 hours
country.countries.0.faqs.4.answer:
Itinerary: Baku – Alat – Volcano Dashgil – Kichik Dash – Boyukdash – ascent to the Kyanizadag volcano – Jingirdag – Baku (approx. 70 km one way, March–October, Jeep 4×4).
Climb to the very crater of active mud volcanoes of the Dashgil group and watch the gurgling liquid clay — scenery so unusual that scientists compare it to the surface of Mars.
country.countries.0.faqs.4.question: Extreme Jeep Safari — Gobustan Reserve, 5–9 hours
country.countries.0.faqs.5.answer:
Itinerary: Baku – Shamakhi – Gabala – Baku (approx. 220 km one way, all season, 10–11 hours).
Gabala overlooks Bazardyuzyu, the highest peak of Azerbaijan (4,466 m). Visit the ruins of ancient Kabalaka — for 600 years the capital of Caucasian Albania — the ancient village of Nij with its Albanian temple, Tufandag summer–winter complex and ropeway, Nohur Lake, and the “Seven Beauties” waterfall.
country.countries.0.faqs.5.question: Tour to Gabala — the capital of Caucasian Albania, 1 day
country.countries.0.faqs.6.answer:
Itinerary: Baku – Guba – Khinalig – Baku (approx. 220 km one way, all season, 10–12 hours).
Khinalig is the oldest settlement in Azerbaijan, over 5,000 years old, whose residents consider themselves descendants of the biblical Noah. On the way explore Guba — famous for its apples, carpet weaving, the Gechresh forest, Tangaalti gorge, Afurdja waterfall, and the Red Village, home to one of the world’s largest communities of Mountain Jews.
country.countries.0.faqs.6.question: Tour to Khinalig — 2,200 meters above sea level, 1 day
country.countries.0.faqs.7.answer:
Itinerary: Baku – Shemakha – Ismayilli – Gabala – Oguz – Sheki – Baku (approx. 320 km one way, all season, 12–13 hours).
Sheki is one of the oldest settlements in the Caucasus with findings dating back more than 2,500 years. See the majestic Sheki Khan’s Palace built without a single nail, the ancient Albanian temple in Kish, Gelersen-Gerersen fortress, caravanserais, Juma Mosque, and medieval baths. Optional stops include the Diri Baba Mausoleum and Nohur Lake.
country.countries.0.faqs.7.question: Tour to Sheki — Ancient Khanate, 1 day
country.countries.0.faqs.8.answer:
Itinerary: Baku – Gusar – Shahdag – Laza – Baku (approx. 220 km one way, 10–12 hours).
09:30 departure from Baku with a stop near Beshbarmag Mountain; arched bridge across Gudiyalchay river and the Red Village on the way. Arrival at the Shahdag summer–winter tourism complex — Azerbaijan’s first ski resort — then a trip to Laza village, an exotic area with majestic waterfalls and horse-riding opportunities.
country.countries.0.faqs.8.question: “The North Gates” Tour — Gusar + Shahdag + Laza, 1 day
country.countries.0.faqs.9.answer:
Itinerary: Baku – Guba – Baku (approx. 220 km one way, all season, 10–12 hours).
Highlights: Guba fruit gardens, the fabled Khinalig village, Chanlibel Lake, Guba Genocide Memorial Complex, Qechresh Forest, “Nazli Bulag” spring, “Mest Dergah” waterfall, Red Town, the Guba carpet workshop, Azerbaijan National Golf Club, Guba bazaar and sweet factory, and the Shahdag Tourism Complex with cable car rides and skiing.
country.countries.0.faqs.9.question: Tour to the North — Forests and Mountains of Quba, 1 day

country.countries.10.description: Ukraine, a land of fertile plains, majestic mountains, and vibrant rivers, reflects centuries of culture through its cities and countryside. Kyiv’s golden domes, Lviv’s charming streets, and the Carpathian Mountains highlight its natural beauty. The country is known for resilience, hospitality, and rich folk heritage.
country.countries.10.faqs.0.answer:
Day 1 (walking): Poshtova Ploshcha, a ride on the Kyiv Metro to Arsenalna — the deepest metro station in the world (105.5 m), the Motherland Monument, the WWII History Museum, Kyiv Pechersk Lavra, Khreshchatyk, Independence Square, Sofia’s Square, the funicular, Andrew’s Descent, and the Landscape Alley.
Day 2 (by car): Shchekavytsia mountain, Heydar Aliyev Square, Khreshchaty Park with the People’s Friendship Arch, the Golden Gate, and the National Botanical Garden.
country.countries.10.faqs.0.question: Day 1–2 — Kyiv walking & city tour
country.countries.10.faqs.1.answer:
Day 3 (shopping): Globus Mall, Ocean Plaza, Gulliver Mall, the Bessarabian market, and Lavina Mall.
Day 4: the Museum of Folk Architecture, Feofaniya Park, and Mezhyhirya National Park.
Day 5: the “12 Months” contact zoo, Pushcha-Vodytsia Park, and Koncha-Zaspa Park.
country.countries.10.faqs.1.question: Day 3–5 — Shopping & around Kyiv
country.countries.10.faqs.2.answer:
Day 6: the Uman district.
Day 7 (Carpathian Mountains): Vysokyi Verkh mountain and cable car, Synevyr Lake — the largest lake in the Ukrainian Carpathians — and Bukovel, the largest ski resort in Eastern Europe.
Day 8 (Lviv): the Theatre of Opera and Ballet, Market Square with the Town Hall, Black House, Venetian House (Chocolate Museum) and Pharmacy Museum, High Castle Hill, and the Vernissage market.

country.countries.4.description: Kazakhstan, the heart of the Great Steppes, stretches across vast plains, deserts, serene lakes, and towering mountains. Here, nomadic heritage blends with modern urban life, creating a dynamic and multicultural society. Travelers are fascinated by its breathtaking nature, nomadic exoticism, and a striking modern contrast.
country.countries.4.faqs.0.answer:
Day 1: transfer from Almaty International Airport, Almaty city tour, Arbat Street, President Park, and a national dinner at Alasha restaurant.
Day 2: Ile-Alatau National Park, Alma-Arasan village, the Big Almatinka–Prokhodnaya river and spring, Zailiyskiy Alatau Mountain, Big Almaty Lake, lunch at Alpen Rose Resort, and a birds show.

country.countries.4.faqs.1.answer:

Day 4: Issyk Lake with boat tours, lunch at Stetson Rancho horse center, the river, and Turgen (Ayuly) waterfalls.
country.countries.4.faqs.1.question: Day 3–4 — Shymbulak, Medeu & Issyk Lake
country.countries.4.faqs.2.answer:

country.countries.5.description: Uzbekistan, a jewel of the Great Silk Road, boasts cities like Samarkand, Bukhara, and Khiva, where stunning architecture and vibrant arts tell the story of a thriving past. Rich traditions and colorful history make every visit unforgettable. Travelers enjoy its history, art, and ancient culture.
country.countries.5.faqs.0.answer: Tashkent downtown walking street, city park, Chorsu Bazaar, Besh Qozon plov center, the TV tower, Timur and Independence squares, Chimgan Mountains, Charvak Lake, panorama cable car, Khast-Imam Ensemble, Barak Khan Madrasah, Juma Mosque, Kukeldash Madrasah, Minor Mosque, and the State Museum of History of Uzbekistan.
country.countries.5.faqs.0.question: Tashkent Tour
country.countries.5.faqs.1.answer: Shah-i-Zinda, Siab Bazaar, Bibi-Khanym Mosque, Registan Square with the Sher-Dor, Ulugbek, and Tillya-Kori madrasahs, and the Gur-Emir Mausoleum.
country.countries.5.faqs.1.question: Samarkand Tour
country.countries.5.faqs.2.answer: Chimgan Mountains, the Chimgan cable car, mountain lakes, horseback riding, and the Amirsoy ski resort.
country.countries.5.faqs.2.question: Amirsoy Tour
country.countries.5.faqs.3.answer: Old Bukhara: the Samanid Mausoleum, the Ark of Bukhara, Mir-i-Arab Madrasa, Toqi Zargaron, Ulugbek and Abdulaziz Khan madrasahs, Taqi-Telpakfurushon market, the Lyabi-Hauz ensemble, Toqi Sarrofon bazaar, Poi Kalyan Mosque, Nodir Devon Begi madrasah and khanaka, and Magoki Attor Mosque.
country.countries.5.faqs.3.question: Bukhara Tour
country.countries.5.faqs.4.answer: Kalta Minor Minaret, Muhammad Amin-khan Madrasah, Islam Khodja Minaret, Juma Mosque, the walls of Itchan Kala, Tash-Hauli Palace, Mahmud Pahlavan Mausoleum, the watchtower of Kunya-Ark Citadel, and Allakuli Khan Madrasah.

The country is dominated by dramatic mountain ranges, alpine meadows, glaciers, and high-altitude lakes. Kyrgyzstan preserves strong traditions of oral heritage, horsemanship, and communal values.

country.countries.7.description: Spanning Eastern Europe and Northern Asia, Russia is one of the world’s most geographically vast and culturally complex countries. Its landscapes range from tundra and taiga forests to mountains, rivers, steppes, and Arctic coastlines. Russia is known for its cultural depth, literary tradition, and astonishing natural landscapes.
country.countries.7.faqs.0.answer:
City tour: Moscow cable car, the Moscow City 360 observation deck, Afimall, and Arbat Street.
Downtown walking day: the Red Square, St. Basil's Cathedral, the Kremlin, Alexander Garden, Zaryadye Park, GUM, and Nikolskaya Street.
Also: Tsaritsyno Royal Park, Vegas Mall, the Moscow Oceanarium, Izmailovo Kremlin, Moscow Cathedral Mosque, the zoo, a Moscow river cruise, plus exclusive extras — helicopter tours, the Bolshoi Theatre, and the Tretyakov Gallery.
country.countries.7.faqs.0.question: Moscow Tours
country.countries.7.faqs.1.answer:
City tour: the Hermitage, Nevsky Avenue walking tour, the Sweet House, the Chocolate Museum, a boat tour, and a Russian folk dance show in a palace.
Outside tour: Peterhof Park, Fabergé Museum, the botanic garden and zoo, shopping, and the famous bridge-raising tour. Extras: the porcelain factory, balloon flights, museum and roof tours, and private boat trips.
country.countries.7.faqs.1.question: St. Petersburg Tour
country.countries.7.faqs.2.answer:

country.countries.9.description: Located in the heart of Europe, Poland has a long and complex history marked by resilience and cultural continuity. Its landscapes include vast plains, forests, lakes, and a Baltic Sea coastline, as well as mountain ranges in the south. Poland is rich in historical landmarks, intellectual traditions, and cultural heritage.
country.countries.9.faqs.0.answer: Walking tour of the old town: St. Florian’s Gate, Wawel Hill with the Royal Castle and Cathedral, the Main Market Square, Kazimierz with New Square and Szeroka Street, St. Mary’s Basilica, handmade souvenir and antique shops, the Jagiellonian University district, Planty Park, and Energylandia. Optional sightseeing by electric golf car covering the Cloth Hall, Town Hall, Barbican, the synagogues of Kazimierz, Ghetto Heroes Square, and Oskar Schindler’s Factory.
country.countries.9.faqs.0.question: Krakow City Tour
country.countries.9.faqs.1.answer: Gubalowka funicular, the “Wielka Krokiew” ski jump, Krupowki Street, Willa Koliba — the Museum of Zakopane Style, Koscieliska street and valley with optional horse-and-carriage ride, Kalatowki Valley, the Regional Museum, thermal pools, and traditional mountaineers’ cottages.
country.countries.9.faqs.1.question: Zakopane Tour
country.countries.9.faqs.2.answer: Wieliczka Salt Mine — one of the first 12 UNESCO World Heritage Sites in the world: a 2-hour route through winding corridors, 800 steps, and a descent to 135 meters underground, with statues and chandeliers carved directly in salt rock. Bochnia Salt Mine: underground vaults with a railway ride, the world’s longest underground slide, a ferry on the brine lake, and the Chapel of Saint Kinga at 212 meters underground.
country.countries.9.faqs.2.question: Wieliczka & Bochnia Salt Mines
country.countries.9.faqs.3.answer: Visit the largest concentration camp of the Nazi Holocaust with a professional guide: the infamous “Arbeit Macht Frei” gate, the barracks and gas chambers, both Auschwitz I and Auschwitz II-Birkenau, and the wailing wall where visitors pay their respects to the victims.
country.countries.9.faqs.3.question: Auschwitz-Birkenau Memorial and Museum
country.countries.9.faqs.4.answer: Trace the story of Krakow’s Jewish community through the centuries: explore Kazimierz with its synagogues, monuments and kosher restaurants, visit the Old Synagogue and Remuh Synagogue and Cemetery, hear about the WWII ghetto, see locations where Steven Spielberg filmed Schindler’s List, and visit the Oskar Schindler’s Factory museum.
country.countries.9.faqs.4.question: Jewish Quarter Walk — 4 hours
country.countries.9.faqs.5.answer: Small art galleries with handmade products and original Polish design, coffee with an urban artisan specializing in amber jewelry — learn to tell real amber from fakes — unique clothes and shoes made in Krakow by local designers, and local pottery traditions.

tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.0.excerpt: Golf holidays at The National Azerbaijan Golf Club and Dreamland Golf Club, with special packages from 500 USD.
The National Azerbaijan Golf Club is located in the area of the Guba Palace Hotel in the north-west of Azerbaijan, sitting at the foot of the breathtaking Caucasus Mountains. Designed by Jon Hunt from International Golf Design, this was the first golf course to open in the “land of fire.” During the summer of 2014, less than two months after its official unveiling, it hosted the country’s inaugural professional event — the Azerbaijan Challenge Open.
A fully equipped clubhouse offers luxurious locker rooms, a professional shop, and a truly unique dining experience. Positioned within 75 hectares of idyllic Azerbaijani topography, the undulating fairways and deep bunkers create a lasting impression, while accessible landing zones and greens give a “look hard, play easy” feel. Distance from Baku to Guba: 168 km.
At Dreamland you will find everything about the Dye Course — from green fees and hire charges to hole-by-hole flyover videos. The luxury clubhouse is a place to eat, drink and socialize, with a golf shop, dress code, and the hole-in-one Roll of Honour. Dreamland is an IMG Golf club. Distance from Baku: 22 km.
Guba Palace Hotel 5*, Deluxe King room with mountain view.
Includes: 3 nights accommodation in Guba, buffet breakfast, 3 rounds of golf at The National Golf Club Guba, private car with driver, golf car, Guba city tour, Shahdag or Khinalig Village tour, all transfers (Baku–Guba–Baku), and all taxes.
Special Golf Package — 3 nights in Guba (from 540 USD per pax)
Accommodation: Fairmont Hotel 5* (Deluxe King City View), Dreamland Hotel 4*, or Mercure Baku City Hotel 5* (from 500 USD per pax).
Program: Day 1 — transfer to hotel; Day 2 — Baku city tour; Day 3 — 1 round of golf at Dreamland Golf Club and Absheron tour; Day 4 — transfer to airport.
Includes: 3 nights accommodation, buffet breakfast, 1 round of golf, private car with driver, golf car, Baku city tour, Absheron tour, all transfers and taxes.
Not included: flight tickets, visa, meals and drinks, personal expenditures, guide.

tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.1.excerpt: Full-day trip to the north of Azerbaijan: Beshbarmag Mountain, Shahdag resort, and the waterfalls of Laza.
Pick-up & drop-off at your hotel (inside Baku)
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.1.packages.0: Economy package: professional driver, personal comfortable transportation during tours (Mercedes Viano/Vito), and guide service.
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.1.packages.1: VIP package: a wide range of luxury cars (Mercedes S class, E class, V class, etc.).
09:30 — departure from Baku to Gusar with a stop near Beshbarmag Mountain. On the way you will see the arched bridge across the Gudiyalchay river and the Red Village in Guba district, home to one of the largest communities of Mountain Jews in the world.
13:00–14:00 — dinner at a local restaurant (for extra payment).
15:00 — arrival at the summer & winter touristic complex Shahdag.
16:00 — trip to Laza, an exotic area with a large number of waterfalls and an opportunity for horse riding.
17:00–20:00 — departure to Baku.
Itinerary: Baku – Gusar – Shahdag – Laza – Baku
Gusar is the last major settlement in the north of Azerbaijan, called “The North Gates” as it borders the Republic of Dagestan. The population consists of Lezgins — an original nationality with a rich culture known for hospitality. Four of the nine climatic zones are found in Gusar. The region is famous for the Shahdag summer–winter complex, operating all year round: a popular ski resort in winter and a camp site on the slopes of the Greater Caucasus in summer. Part of the territory belongs to Shahdag National Park — the largest in Azerbaijan. Historical monuments include the fortress walls in Enih village (13th century), the 1544 tomb of Sheikh Juneyd, and the 18th-century mosques of Kohne Khudat and Khuray. The folklore ensemble “Lezginka” keeps alive the famous Caucasian dance of the same name.
Laza village lies on the north-east outskirts of the Main Caucasian Ridge, at the foot of the “Shah Yaylag” plateau and the 4,242 m Shahdag peak, at an altitude of 1,300 m on an ancient caravan route. Its population consists mostly of ethnic Lezgins whose history is closely connected with Caucasian Albania, one of the most ancient states on the territory of Azerbaijan. In winter most of the waterfalls freeze and climbing competitions are organized on the frozen falls.
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.1.title: “The North Gates” Tour — Gusar + Shahdag + Laza
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.10.excerpt: Maiden Tower, Shirvanshah's Palace, and medieval caravanserais of Icheri Sheher on foot.
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.10.inclusions: Pick-up & drop-off at your hotel (inside Baku)
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.10.packages.0: Economy package: professional driver, personal comfortable transportation during tours (Mercedes Viano/Vito), and guide service.
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.10.packages.1: VIP package: a wide range of luxury cars (Mercedes S class, E class, V class, etc.).
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.10.sections.0: Understanding the development of medieval Icheri Sheher and urban life in Baku is key to understanding the current economic, material, and moral culture of present-day Azerbaijan.
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.10.sections.1:
Maiden Tower — the symbol of Baku in the southeastern part of Icheri Sheher.
Juma Mosque (12th century) — a center of the social, political, and cultural life of medieval Azerbaijan.
Old caravanserais — the 14th-century Multani Caravanserai on Qulle Street and the Bukhara Caravanserai opposite, shelters for merchants on the caravan routes.
Bathhouses — half-underground hammams, warm in winter and cool in summer.
Khanegahs — dwellings of the Sufis along the trade routes.
Shirvanshah’s Palace (12th–15th century) — the last residence of the rulers of Shirvan, built after the capital moved to Baku following the 1191 earthquake.
Museum of Miniature Books — 4,350 miniature books from 62 countries on display.
The Mugham Theatre — a 15th-century two-floored caravanserai with an octagonal courtyard.
Fountain Square, the National History Museum, a sea trip along Baku Bay, and Baku’s largest shopping malls.
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.10.title: Old City Excursion (Walking Tour)
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.11.excerpt: A gastronomic journey: tandir breakfast, the eastern bazaar, sturgeon kebab, and shah-plov.
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.11.inclusions: Pick-up & drop-off at your hotel (inside Baku)
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.11.packages.0: Economy package: professional driver, personal comfortable transportation during tours (Mercedes Viano/Vito), and guide service.
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.11.packages.1: VIP package: a wide range of luxury cars (Mercedes S class, E class, V class, etc.).
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.11.sections.0: Spend a day like a real Baku citizen and try the best dishes in authentic restaurants. This excursion opens the world of the richest Azerbaijani cuisine: a gastronomic journey through the Old City, the colorful Baku bazaar, and the coast of the Caspian Sea. In one day you will learn new shades of flavors and try local delicacies — the most delicious lamb and sturgeon, fragrant wines, countless spices, and dried fruits.
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.11.sections.1:
Breakfast in the Old Town — rustic butter, cheese, honey, hot cakes freshly baked in tandir, tea or coffee.
The eastern bazaar — one of the oldest Baku bazaars: the specifics of Azerbaijani cuisine, its main ingredients and spices; taste and buy spices, dried fruits, fresh Caspian black caviar, and exclusive Azerbaijani teas.
Lunch — shish kebab from freshly caught sturgeon or lamb, shah-plov, and other traditional dishes at a traditional restaurant.
Authentic dishes and secret recipes — a stroll through the iconic sights of the Old Town with tea or wine tasting.
Organizational details — food and drinks are paid separately; transportation is included. Tell us your culinary preferences in advance.
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.11.title: Tastes and Flavors of Azerbaijani Cuisine
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.12.excerpt: Ancient Kabalaka, the Tufandag ropeway, Nohur Lake, and the Seven Beauties waterfall.
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.12.inclusions: Pick-up & drop-off at your hotel (inside Baku)
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.12.packages.0: Economy package: professional driver, personal comfortable transportation during tours (Mercedes Viano/Vito), and guide service.
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.12.packages.1: VIP package: a wide range of luxury cars (Mercedes S class, E class, V class, etc.).
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.12.sections.0: Gabala is a unique and beautiful region on the bank of the Demiraparanchay River, overlooking the highest peak of Azerbaijan — Bazardyuzyu (4,466 m). The main sight is the ruins of an ancient town 15 km from the city center: “Kabalaka,” mentioned in 1st-century historical sources and for 600 years the capital of Caucasian Albania.
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.12.sections.1: The ancient village of Nij with its Albanian temple (4th–7th centuries) — homeland of the Azerbaijani-Udi ethnic group, descendants of the Caucasian Albanians; the ancient defensive tower (9th–11th centuries); and Ustajan Tower (9th–14th centuries). Modern hotel and resort infrastructure serves the most demanding travelers, and every summer Gabala hosts international music festivals.
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.12.sections.2: The ancient town of Kabalaka, Gabala Land entertainment center, the Tufandag summer–winter touristic complex, the ropeway, Nij village and its Albanian temple, Gabala Shooting Center, Nohur Lake, and the “Seven Beauties” waterfall.
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.12.title: Tour to Gabala — the Capital of Caucasian Albania
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.13.excerpt: The 5,000-year-old mountain village of Noah's descendants, via Guba and the Caucasus gorges.
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.13.inclusions: Pick-up & drop-off at your hotel (inside Baku)
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.13.packages.0: Economy package: professional driver, personal comfortable transportation during tours (Mercedes Viano/Vito), and guide service.
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.13.packages.1: VIP package: a wide range of luxury cars (Mercedes S class, E class, V class, etc.).
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.13.sections.0: Khinalig is the oldest settlement in Azerbaijan, on the northern slope of the Main Caucasian Ridge at an altitude of 2,100–2,200 m. Its residents consider themselves direct descendants of the biblical Noah, and the village’s history goes back more than 5,000 years. For centuries it was cut off from civilization by hard-to-reach mountains with dangerous rocky cliffs.
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.13.sections.1: Today there are about 380 houses, 200–300 years old, built so closely on the steep slopes that the roof of one house serves as the patio of the one above. The inhabitants have retained their traditional way of life, culture, and original language.
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.13.sections.2: Guba district is a favorite destination 168 km from Baku: the shady Gechresh forest, Tangaalti gorge, the Velvelichay river, and the famous Afurdja waterfall, listed among the “Nature Monuments of Azerbaijan.” The Red Village here is home to one of the largest communities of Mountain Jews in the world. Guba is famous for its apples and is a recognized carpet-weaving center; it was visited by Alexandre Dumas, Bestuzhev-Marlinsky, and Thor Heyerdahl.
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.13.title: Tour to Khinalig — 2,200 Meters Above Sea Level
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.14.excerpt: Apple orchards, the Red Town, mountain waterfalls, and the Shahdag complex in one day.
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.14.inclusions: Pick-up & drop-off at your hotel (inside Baku)
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.14.packages.0: Economy package: professional driver, personal comfortable transportation during tours (Mercedes Viano/Vito), and guide service.
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.14.packages.1: VIP package: a wide range of luxury cars (Mercedes S class, E class, V class, etc.).
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.14.sections.0:
09:30 — departure from Baku to Guba with a stop near Beshbarmag Mountain; the arched bridge across the Gudiyalchay river and the Red Village on the way.
13:00–14:00 — dinner at a local restaurant (extra payment).
15:00 — arrival at the Shahdag summer & winter touristic complex.
16:00 — trip to Laza, an exotic area with many waterfalls and horse-riding opportunities.
17:00–20:00 — departure to Baku.
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.14.sections.1: Famous for apples and carpet-making, Guba is one of the major and oldest cities of Azerbaijan, 165 km north of Baku, with unique nature, a pleasant climate, and gorgeous air. It is a popular getaway and the gateway to Azerbaijan’s most interesting remote mountain villages.
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.14.sections.2: Guba fruit gardens fed by crystal-clear melt water; presentation of the national sweet “Bukme”; the fabled village of Khinalig; Chanlibel Lake — one of the most beautiful lakes of Azerbaijan; the Guba Genocide Memorial Complex; Qechresh Forest with its natural springs; the “Nazli Bulag” spring; the “Mest Dergah” waterfall with wooden cottages; the Red Town — primary settlement of Mountain Jews (about 4,000 people); the Guba carpet workshop; Azerbaijan National Golf Club — the first golf course of the “land of fire”; Guba bazaar; the Guba sweet factory; and the Shahdag Tourism Complex with cable car rides and skiing.
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.14.title: Tour to the North — Forests and Mountains of Quba
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.15.excerpt: Medieval coppersmiths' town with cobbled streets, via the ancient capital Shemakha.
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.15.inclusions: Pick-up & drop-off at your hotel (inside Baku)
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.15.packages.0: Economy package: professional driver, personal comfortable transportation during tours (Mercedes Viano/Vito), and guide service.
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.15.packages.1: VIP package: a wide range of luxury cars (Mercedes S class, E class, V class, etc.).
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.15.sections.0: Shemakha is an ancient capital of Azerbaijan, founded in the 5th century BC. In the 9th–16th centuries it was the capital of the Shirvan khanate, residence of the Shirvanshahs, and one of the most beautiful cities of the East. The main sight is the 11th–12th-century Gulistan fortress; also the Seven Domes (Yeddi Gumbez) mausoleum, the 10th-century Juma Mosque — the oldest in the Caucasus after Derbent — and ruins from the 10th–17th centuries. Today Shemakha is a winemaking and carpet-weaving center and the hometown of many Azerbaijani poets.
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.15.sections.1: The high-altitude village of Lahij in Ismayilli region is an original monument of ancient architecture, founded in the 3rd–4th century BC. In this medieval town, famous far beyond the Caucasus, cobbled streets and ancient squares are preserved in their original form. Here you will find copper, leather, and wood articles, peculiar samples of cold steel, and many workshops where hereditary blacksmiths still create household items — you can watch the process and buy freshly made goods. Remarkably, the water and sewage system was built more than 1,500 years ago, and no one knows where it starts, where it ends, or how it works.
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.15.title: Tour to the Village of Artisans — Lahij

Itinerary: Absheron – Ateshgah – Yanar Dag – Gala
Heydar Aliyev Center — the 619,000-square-foot complex designed by architect Zaha Hadid.
Ateshgah Temple — the Temple of Eternal Fire.
Yanardag — the eternally burning mountain.
“Gala” Archaeological and Ethnographic Museum Complex — an outdoor museum.
Bina Equestrian Center — home of the Karabakh horse, a mountain-steppe racing and riding breed found only in Azerbaijan.
Heydar Mosque — the biggest mosque in the Caucasus.
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.2.title: Absheron Tour

Your personal guide will help you design the perfect 2-hour itinerary, escorting you to Baku attractions and offering insider tips on where to go and what to see in the capital. Visit the main attractions and request stops to snap photos, grab a souvenir, or take a stroll around your favorite spots.
Highland Park — panoramic view of Baku Bay from the highest point of the city.
National Flag Square — one of the highest flags in the world.
Flame Towers — the trio of skyscrapers that defines Baku’s skyline.
The Funicular — Baku’s modernized funicular system.
Carpet Museum — the State Museum of Azerbaijani Carpets and Applied Arts.
Baku Boulevard (Seaside Park) — the promenade along Baku’s seafront.
Little Venice — the water park in Baku.
Government House, the State Philharmonic Hall, and Fountain Square — the most famous street of Baku.

Museum of Petroglyphs (outdoor museum) — Gobustan is located approximately 65 km from Baku. Prehistoric rock drawings — petroglyphs — are an art “archive” of human evolution on Earth. The largest such outdoor archive in Azerbaijan is in Gobustan, at the Baku State Reserve of History, Ethnography and Arts, near the Caspian Sea on the ancient Shirvan road. In the mountains of Beyukdash, Kichikdash, Djingirdag and Shighgaya are concentrated rock paintings, ancient camps, and tombstones from the Stone Age onward, reflecting the culture, economy, worldview, customs, and traditions of the ancient peoples of Azerbaijan.

Sheki is an ancient historical city in the southern foothills of the Greater Caucasus, 300 km north-west of Baku. Archaeological evidence suggests Sheki is one of the oldest settlements in the Caucasus, with findings dating back more than 2,500 years.
Itinerary: Baku – Shemakha – Ismayilli – Gabala – Oguz – Sheki – Baku
Many historical monuments are preserved in their original form. The main sight is the majestic Sheki Khan’s Palace with its magnificent wall paintings, constructed in the 18th century without using a single nail. Other popular places: the ancient Albanian temple in Kish settlement, Gelersen-Gerersen fortress (8th–9th centuries), numerous caravanserais, the house of Sheki Khans, Juma Mosque (18th century), the minaret of the Gileili mosque, medieval baths, the theater, the historical museum, and the house-museum of writer M. F. Akhundov.
Diri Baba Mausoleum, Juma Mosque in Shemakha, the ancient city of Kabalaka, the Tufandag summer–winter complex, Nij village with its ancient Albanian temple, Nohur Lake, the Palace of Sheki Khans, and the caravanserai.

tours.byCountry.poland.2.sections.0.body: The Wieliczka Salt Mine was one of the first 12 sites in the world designated a UNESCO World Heritage Site and is one of the most visited tourist sites anywhere. The 2-hour tour covers only 1% of the underground world: winding corridors almost 3 km long, 800 steps, and a descent to a depth of 135 meters through the Danilowicz Shaft. Explore a vast labyrinth of tunnels and chambers and admire intricate statues, chandeliers, and other art carved directly in the salt rock.
tours.byCountry.poland.2.sections.0.head: Wieliczka Salt Mine
tours.byCountry.poland.2.sections.1.body: Hear the story of Duke Boleslaw V the Chaste and Duchess Kinga, ride the underground railway, go down the world’s longest underground slide, and sail a ferry on the brine lake flooding the chamber. Visit a network of chambers with unusual geological structures, a stable, an exhibition of wooden tread-wheels, and the Chapel of Saint Kinga at a depth of 212 meters underground.

tours.byCountry.ukraine.0.excerpt: 8-day program: Pechersk Lavra, the Carpathians, Bukovel, and Lviv.
tours.byCountry.ukraine.0.meta.0.label: Duration
tours.byCountry.ukraine.0.meta.1.label: Cities
tours.byCountry.ukraine.0.sections.0.bod: A walk to Poshtova Ploshcha (the Dnieper River station) and a ride on the Kyiv Metro to the deepest metro station in the world — Arsenalna (105.5 m below the surface). The Motherland Monument (102 m), the Museum of the History of Ukraine in WWII, the Park of Eternal Glory, Kyiv Pechersk Lavra, Khreshchatyk — the main street of Kyiv — the Kyiv Passage, Independence Square (Maidan Nezalezhnosti), Sofia’s Square, the Kyiv funicular (Saint Volodymyr Hill), Andrew’s Descent, and the Landscape Alley.
tours.byCountry.ukraine.0.sections.1.bod: Shchekavytsia mountain, Heydar Aliyev Square, Khreshchaty Park with the People’s Friendship Arch, the Golden Gate, and the National Botanical Garden.
tours.byCountry.ukraine.0.sections.2.bod: Globus Mall, Ocean Plaza, Gulliver Mall, the Bessarabian market, and Lavina Mall.
tours.byCountry.ukraine.0.sections.3.bod:
Day 4: the Museum of Folk Architecture and Folkways of Ukraine, Feofaniya Park, and Mezhyhirya National Park (the former residence of the fourth President of Ukraine).
Day 5: the “12 Months” contact zoo, Pushcha-Vodytsia Park, and Koncha-Zaspa Park.
tours.byCountry.ukraine.0.sections.4.bod:
Day 6: the Uman district.
Day 7: Vysokyi Verkh mountain with its cable car, Synevyr Lake — the largest lake in the Ukrainian Carpathians — and the resort of Bukovel, the largest ski resort in Eastern Europe.
tours.byCountry.ukraine.0.sections.5.bod: The Lviv Theatre of Opera and Ballet; Market Square with its 45 old architectural buildings and 4 fountains — the Town Hall, the Black House, the Venetian House (Chocolate Museum), and the Pharmacy Museum; High Castle Hill; and the Vernissage market.
tours.byCountry.ukraine.0.title: Kyiv City Tour
